meaning: n.[会计]分类帐,分户总帐,底帐,[建](手脚架上的)横木
examples: She closed the ledger with a smack.
That would be the Joker, of course, a demonic creation and the three-ring circus of one wholly inhabited by Heath Ledger.
Without this enclosure, but just within the street entrance, sat a grey-haired gentleman at a small table, with a large open ledger of some kind before him.
On the other side of the technical ledger, surging bond takeout activity helped tamp down supply.
However, the key to success is not simply a review of the numbers on a ledger.
